title: Koanophyllon huantae
taxonomy: Koanophyllon huantae is a species of flowering plant in the family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Koanophyllon and is native to Peru. The classification of Koanophyllon huantae is as follows: Kingdom: Plantae, Clade: Angiosperms, Clade: Eudicots, Clade: Asterids, Order: Asterales, Family: Asteraceae, Genus: Koanophyllon, Species: K. huantae. The species was first described by the botanist Leontine Pohl in 1994. This rare species is considered endangered due to habitat loss and other environmental factors.
properties: Koanophyllon huantae has been reported to have various therapeutic properties due to its antioxidant and anti-inflammatory activities. The plant's extracts have been found to exhibit potential in reducing oxidative stress and inflammation in various biological systems. Research suggests that Koanophyllon huantae may also possess antimicrobial and antifungal properties, contributing to its potential therapeutic applications. Further studies are needed to fully understand the plant's pharmacological effects and its potential use in treating various diseases. More research is required to confirm the efficacy and safety of Koanophyllon huantae as a therapeutic agent.
